staff.advice:
• **Redistribute Grace's classes immediately**: Her Build: Lower (17 sessions) should shift to JoJo; Elevate (6) and Strongevity (3) to Eilis. This removes Grace from the timetable and uses JoJo's strength in structured lifting and Eilis's proven Elevate experience.

• **Move Grace's PT (0 sessions going forward) and reduce her InBody scans**: Her 9 InBody scans can be handled by JoJo or Eilis in spare slots—they're admin, not coaching. This frees Grace entirely.

• **Rebalance JoJo's PT load**: At 44 PTs, JoJo is over-loaded. Shift 8–10 of these to Eilis to build her confidence and create headroom for JoJo to lead Tuesdays without burnout. Eilis currently has only 11 PTs and capacity to grow.

• **Close the No Sweat Intro gap**: Grace ran 3; these should rotate between JoJo and Eilis on a monthly rota to avoid single-person dependency and give both exposure to new-member onboarding.

• **Plug Mobility and Circuits**: JoJo covers both (7 + 6 sessions). Consider whether Eilis can lead Mobility once confident, or whether these low-frequency classes can be consolidated or guest-coached to ease JoJo's timetable.

• **Cap JoJo at ~115 sessions/month post-rebalance**: Current 133 is unsustainable for a head coach. Shift PT and Lower-body Build to Eilis, keep JoJo's Tuesday technical classes and upper-body specialism, and protect her capacity for quality delivery and team support.

jojo@theforge.pt.name: Standardised Warmups and Cooldowns
jojo@theforge.pt.notes:
I have completed Mondays and part of tuesday (feel free to edit or change though).

All days need a standard warmup and cooldown for each quarter so we can print and laminate these and keep in the studio to keep each day consistent. The only caveat to this is if the weather is good then the coach can choose the warmup/cooldown for outside. 

Can you complete the rest of the week: 
https://canva.link/rdamphvgb76et2s

Then add the movement demos here: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1FtozUO4hG5VZDvtR0SmvP5mGKCrJUrLHyQQ0vmHHZCo/edit?usp=sharing

They then need printed and laminated and brought into the studio and put into the coaches draw or in the kitchen cabinet within easy reach. 


We will start these Monday 18th May to give every coach all of next week to review them.

class_stats.suggestion:
**For quiet studio time gaps:**

1. **Add a 30-min "Open Lift" slot on Monday and Thursday evenings** (your quietest days) between PT blocks — members can drop in to use equipment unsupervised or book a solo coaching session, creating revenue from dead time and giving flexible-schedule members an alternative to set classes.

2. **Run a monthly "Intro to Engine" workshop on a quiet Thursday morning** — Engine is severely underattended (15 last month); a 45-min guided intro explaining the format, pacing strategy, and why it complements Build classes could convert curious members who find the standalone class intimidating.

**To lift the quietest classes:**

3. **Move Barbelle and Strongevity to Wednesday (your busiest day) and promote them as "strength pairing" add-ons** — send targeted invites to your Build: Upper/Lower regulars 48 hours before, positioning these as complementary sessions; low attendance suggests positioning/timing issue, not demand issue.
